payload.notes: Quality-preserving Qwen3.6 27B Q4_0 GGUF tensor-split run on two Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs. Same Q4_0 weights and f16 KV cache as baseline, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Local llama.cpp patch adds gated SYCL async cpy_tensor support so Meta tensor parallel reductions avoid the synchronized fallback. Single B70 Q4_0 baseline was 24.723 tok/s; previous best dual tensor was 26.872 tok/s.

Same Q4_0 weights and f16 KV cache, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Local patches add SYCL async cpy_tensor and an experimental SYCL direct F32 Meta all-reduce hook to avoid the generic aux ADD graph in 2-GPU tensor parallel reductions. payload.notes: Quality-preserving Qwen3.6 27B Q4_0 GGUF tensor-split run on two Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs. Same Q4_0 weights and f16 KV cache, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Local patches add SYCL async cpy_tensor, Qwen recurrent split anchoring, and an experimental direct 2-GPU single-kernel F32 allreduce that peer-reads both partial tensors and writes the sum back to both devices. Low-level SYCL peer read/write stress test passed. Validated over 512 generated tokens, 3 repeats: samples 39.8517, 39.7809, 39.9148 tok/s. Previous quality-preserving Q4_0 TP2 result was 38.621 tok/s; single-B70 baseline was 24.723 tok/s.

payload.notes: Quality-preserving Qwen3.6 27B Q4_0 GGUF tensor-split run on three Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs. Same Q4_0 weights and f16 KV cache, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Local patches add SYCL async cpy_tensor, Qwen recurrent split anchoring, and an experimental direct 2-4 GPU single-kernel F32 allreduce that peer-reads all partial tensors and writes the sum back to each device. Low-level four-device SYCL peer read/write stress test passed. Validated over 512 generated tokens, 3 repeats: samples 41.4110, 41.3034, 41.3872 tok/s. Previous best TP3 Q4_0 result was 38.365 tok/s; best TP2 single-kernel result was 39.849 tok/s; single-B70 baseline was 24.723 tok/s.

payload.notes: Quality-preserving Qwen3.6 27B Q4_0 GGUF tensor-split run on three Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs. Same Q4_0 weights and f16 KV cache, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Uses selector order 2,1,3 so physical B70 2 is the root for the experimental direct 2-4 GPU single-kernel F32 allreduce. Local patches add SYCL async cpy_tensor, Qwen recurrent split anchoring, and peer-read/peer-write allreduce. Low-level four-device SYCL peer stress test passed. Validated over 512 generated tokens, 3 repeats: samples 41.6977, 41.6966, 41.8155 tok/s. Previous root 0,1,2 TP3 result was 41.367 tok/s; best TP2 result was 39.849 tok/s; single-B70 baseline was 24.723 tok/s.

payload.notes: Quality-preserving Qwen3.6 27B Q4_0 GGUF tensor-split run on four Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs. Same Q4_0 weights and f16 KV cache, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Uses experimental direct 2-4 GPU single-kernel F32 allreduce. This is intentionally submitted as a negative scaling datapoint: 4-way tensor split regresses versus the best 3-GPU result because the root-kernel fanout must peer-read and write four partial tensors across 128 small F32 allreduces per generated token. 4-GPU root sweep stayed around 30.76-31.31 tok/s at 128 tokens, so the issue appears to be communication fanout/synchronization rather than a single bad root choice. Best TP3 result was 41.737 tok/s; best TP2 result was 39.849 tok/s; single-B70 baseline was 24.723 tok/s.

payload.notes: Higher-quality Qwen3.6 27B Q8_0 GGUF fallback run on two Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs. This is not FP8. Same llama.cpp/SYCL experimental tensor-parallel path as the Q4_0 work: f16 KV cache, greedy decode, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes, async Meta tensor copy, and single-kernel F32 allreduce. Single-B70 Q8_0 decode at the same 512 prompt / 128 output shape was 15.275 tok/s. Q8_0 TP3/TP4 currently abort during model tensor allocation with GGML_ASSERT(buffer), so TP2 is the usable high-quality GGUF mode for now.

payload.notes: Official Qwen3.6 27B FP8 Safetensors on two Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs using vLLM/XPU. This uses a local experimental XPU path that dequantizes the 128x128 block-FP8 checkpoint after load and requantizes to per-channel FP8 W8A16 because current XPU kernels do not provide native block-FP8 W8A8 GEMM for this checkpoint. Latency was 25.464 s for one 512-token prompt plus 512 generated tokens, so tokSOut is outputTokens / end-to-end latency and includes prefill overhead. Fixed 1 GiB KV cache per GPU was used to avoid vLLM/XPU automatic KV profiling OOM. TP4 at the same shape was slower at 26.606 s.

payload.notes: Qwen3.6 27B Q4_0 GGUF on three Intel Arc Pro B70 32GB GPUs using llama.cpp SYCL Level Zero tensor split. Adds env-gated graph-scoped Q8_1 activation cache (GGML_SYCL_Q8_CACHE=1), reusing the exact same quantized activation within one graph compute. Quality-preserving: no model/weight/sample-quality change, no speculative decode, no power-limit changes. Same shape without cache was 40.937 tok/s; longer 512-output validation with cache was 41.659 tok/s.

payload.notes: Diagnostic FP8 KV-cache run on 4x Intel Arc Pro B70. Static Qwen3.6 27B compressed-tensors FP8 with patched vLLM/XPU FA2, but --kv-cache-dtype fp8 was slower than auto/BF16 KV and vLLM warns FP8 KV may cause accuracy drop without proper scaling. Not recommended for quality-preserving speed.
